secondary_archetype: AI Research Operations / Research Infrastructure TPM

level_signals: Compensation range $365K–$435K USD base, consistent with a senior or staff-level IC at Anthropic
'Several years of experience building technical programs from scratch'
Expected to influence senior technical staff and present recommendations to research leadership
0-to-1 scope ownership with no established playbooks or precedent

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have a background in ML research or engineering with several years of experience building technical programs from scratch, ideally with hands-on exposure to training, evaluation, or large-scale distributed systems
must_haves.qualifier: several years; hands-on exposure to training, evaluation, or large-scale distributed systems preferred

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Are a fast learner who can ramp on unfamiliar technical domains quickly and contribute meaningfully to discussions with researchers
Are resourceful, high-agency, and able to navigate ambiguity and shifting priorities to drive progress in fast-moving research environments
Have a track record of operational ownership of complex technical systems, including monitoring, incident response, and performance optimization
Can reason about technical tradeoffs at depth across model architecture, training infrastructure, evals, or compute efficiency, and translate them into clear decisions for leadership
Have excellent stakeholder management skill and the ability to influence senior technical staff through competence and consistent delivery
Are comfortable with high-stakes environments where decisions impact compute spend, model training timelines, and launch outcomes
Are passionate about the potential impact of AI and are committed to developing safe and beneficial systems
Are excited to redefine what technical program management looks like at the frontier of AI research
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally with hands-on exposure to training, evaluation, or large-scale distributed systems

scope.surface: Research organization — full model development lifecycle including pre-training, post-training, alignment, interpretability, safety, compute, evals, RL environments
scope.business_model: frontier AI research and safety organization (public benefit corporation)
primary_metrics_inferred: Eval readiness and quality at model launches
Operational health of RL training environments
core_responsibilities: Embed deeply within a research domain to understand the technical landscape and identify highest-leverage problems
Move fluidly across research areas like compute, evals, RL environments, and emerging research initiatives
Drive end-to-end execution of complex, ambiguous research initiatives spanning multiple teams
Establish processes and frameworks that bring structure to unstructured research environments
Lead large-scale compute resource planning including allocation, efficiency, and prioritization
Drive eval readiness for model launches by standardizing results and improving tooling
Own execution and operational health of RL environments across major training runs
Equip research leadership to make decisions quickly through technical tradeoff analysis
Act as connective tissue between research, engineering, and product teams

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($365K–$435K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with best-effort commitment and immigration lawyer support
Clear 0-to-1 ownership with defined high-impact research domains (compute, evals, RL environments)
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit DE&I language and imposter syndrome acknowledgment
Direct exposure to frontier AI research at Anthropic's core model development work
red_flags: Role requires on-call/incident response on short notice including weekends — explicit in JD
Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco; not fully remote
Years of experience minimum is not numerically stated — left intentionally vague ('several years'), making seniority bar hard to assess externally
Broad, shifting scope ('surface area will shift over time') may mean unclear ownership or constant context-switching

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range $365K–$435K and scope signals; not stated explicitly)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as public benefit corporation)
location.cities_listed (inferred from HQ mention 'headquartered in San Francisco'; no other cities named for this role)
nice_to_haves: hands-on exposure to training/eval/distributed systems listed as 'ideally' qualifier within a required bullet — treated as soft
ambiguities: 'Several years of experience' is not defined numerically; years_required_min left null. The JD explicitly states 'Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' without specifying a number.

The 'ideally with hands-on exposure to training, evaluation, or large-scale distributed systems' qualifier appears within a must-have bullet but uses 'ideally', making it ambiguous whether it is hard or soft. Extracted as a separate nice-to-have with severity soft.
Compensation stated as 'Annual Salary' range — it is unclear if this is base only or inclusive of other cash components for non-sales roles; treated as base.
